content.full_text:
City of Morro Bay
595 Harbor St. • Morro Bay, CA 93442 • 805-772-1214

September 5, 1990


Jeanette Turvill
Legal Department
Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

RE: Updated Conflict of Interest Code

Dear Ms. Turvill:

Pursuant to our conversation this date, enclosed please find
a copy of City of Morro Bay's proposed updated Conflict of
Interest Code. Please review prior to City Council consideration and adoption, and advise if you have any suggested
changes.

Thank you for your assistance.

Sincerely,

ARDITH DAVIS
City Clerk

Enclosures


CITY HALL                          FIRE DEPARTMENT                    PUBLIC WORKS
595 Harbor Street                  715 Harbor Street                  695 Harbor Street

HARBOR DEPARTMENT                  POLICE DEPARTMENT                  RECREATION AND PARKS
1275 Embarcadero                   850 Morro Bay Blvd.                1001 Kennedy Way

I can see this is a very faint or low-contrast document image, but the text is largely illegible to me. Most of the content appears as very light gray text on a white background, making it impossible for me to accurately transcribe the specific words and content while following your requirement to copy the text verbatim.

Rather than guess or provide inaccurate transcription, I should indicate that:

[Most of document content is illegible due to image contrast/quality]

If you could provide a clearer, higher-contrast version of this document, I would be able to provide an accurate verbatim transcription.

SAMPLE

CONFLICT OF INTEREST CODE FOR

THE CITY OF MORRO BAY

The Political Reform Act (Government Code Section 81000, et
seq.) requires state and local government agencies to adopt and
promulgate conflict of interest codes. The Fair Political
Practices Commission has adopted a regulation (2 Cal. Code of
Regulations., Section 18730) which contains the terms of a
standard conflict of interest code, which can be incorporated by
reference in an agency's code. After public notice and hearing,
it may be amended by the Fair Political Practices Commission to
conform to amendments in the Political Reform Act. Therefore, the
terms of 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18730 and any
amendments to it duly adopted by the Fair Political Practices
Commission are hereby incorporated by reference. This regulation
and the attached Appendix designating officials and employees and
establishing disclosure categories shall constitute the conflict
of interest code of the City of Morro Bay.

Designated employees shall file statements of economic
interests with the City which shall make the statements available
for public inspection and reproduction. (Gov. Code Section
81008). Statements for other positions designated in this
conflict of interest code will be retained by the City.*

*     Form 721 statements for Article 2 filers (including but not
limited to: Mayor, Councilmembers, Planning Commissioners, City
Administrator, City Attorney, and Finance Director the City of
Morro Bay) shall be filed with the city who will make and retain
copies and forward the original the Fair Political Practices
Commission.

SAMPLE

APPENDIX A


Designated Position                    Assigned Disclosure
                                       Category

Administration

   Assistant City Administrator                    1
   Assistant City Clerk                           1
   City Clerk                                     2

Community Development

   Community Development Director                  1
   Associate Planner                              1
   Chief Building Inspector                       2
   Building Inspector                             2

Finance

   Accountant                                     3

Fire

   Fire Chief                                     1
   Fire Captain                                   2

Harbor

   Harbor Director                                1
   Chief Harbor Patrol Officer                    2,3

Police

   Chief of Police                                1
   Police Lieutenant                              2,3

Consultants                                       *

______________

* Consultants shall be included in the list of designated
employees and shall disclose pursuant to the broadest disclosure
category in the code subject to the following limitation:

   The (chief executive officer) may determine in writing that a
particular consultant, although a "designated position," is hired
to perform a range of duties that is limited in scope and thus is
not required to fully comply with the disclosure requirements in
this section. Such written determination shall include a
description of the consultant's duties and, based upon that
description, a statement of the extent of disclosure requirements.
The (chief executive officer's) determination is a public record
and shall be retained for public inspection in the same manner and
location as this conflict of interest code.

The following positions are statutorily required to file Form
721 statements of economic interest pursuant to Government Code
Section 87200. Original statements for these positions are
located at the Fair Political Practices Commission. Copies are
also located with the City:

Mayor
Councilmembers
Planning Commission
City Administrator
City Attorney
Finance Director
(list any other positions which must file Form 721 statements)
